scope_note_changelog.change: Initial PEEL prompt bank per VOCAB_COACH_PROMPT_BANK_GENERATION_PROMPT_V1.md. 228 prompt entries covering all 6 flat topics and all 16 real subtopics (across environment/technology/health/education) x all 4 task types (opinion, advantages_disadvantages, cause_effect_problem_solution, discussion), at least 2 prompts per (topic-or-subtopic x task_type) combination -- most units carry 10 prompts (2 opinion + 2 advantages_disadvantages + 4 cause_effect_problem_solution, covering all 4 angles, + 2 discussion). 4 additional multi-subtopic worked examples (one pair per subtopic-bearing topic: environment::energy_and_resources+pollution, technology::devices_and_innovation+data_and_security, health::lifestyle_and_prevention+mental_health, education::skills_and_curriculum+access_and_equality), each authored as 2 entries (advantage/disadvantage) sharing one scenario_text, drawing vocabulary from the union of both tagged subtopics. Every suggested_vocabulary item is copied verbatim from vocab_coach_topic_bank_v1_3_0.json or vocab_coach_task_type_bank_v1_2_0.json and re-verified by this build script against those live files (see verification section of the addendum) -- none invented or paraphrased. Every instruction_template has an explicit singular-angle marker ('ONE advantage', 'ONE cause', etc.) baked into the wording itself. No CEFR field added to prompt entries (band-gating stays on vocabulary items only, per Requirement 6). Correction to this prompt's own Requirement 8: it asserted 'no vocab_coach_engine*.py or equivalent exists anywhere in the project' -- this is factually incorrect. vocab_coach_engine_v1_0_0.py through vocab_coach_engine_v1_3_0.py already exist in gold/LRET/vocab_coach/, built earlier in this same working session. The substance of Requirement 8 still holds and was honoured: none of those engine files currently read this prompt bank, the topic bank's subtopics/angle-aware selection, or the task-type bank's angle field (a gap already flagged in VOCAB_COACH_BANK_REVISION_V1_addendum.md). This is stated here as 'when an engine is updated to consume this bank, it will need to...', not as though a currently-reading engine exists and would break.

known_limitations: Per unit, only 2 concrete scenario_texts (A and B) are authored and reused across the unit's 10 task-type/angle prompt entries. This keeps the bank internally consistent and dedup-clean ((scenario_text, task_type, angle) is always unique) but means a student who does many sessions on the same topic will eventually see a repeated scenario paired with a new angle/task_type -- this is a deliberate authoring-scale tradeoff, not an oversight, and more scenario variety per unit is a natural v1.1.0 expansion if session volume warrants it.

Task-type vocabulary (opinion/cause-effect/advantages-disadvantages/discussion phrases) is drawn from small rotating pools shared across all 22 units, since task-type phrases are inherently topic-agnostic. The same rhetorical phrase (e.g. 'result in') can appear in suggested_vocabulary for more than one prompt -- this does not violate the dedup rule (which is keyed on scenario_text + task_type + angle, not vocabulary contents) and mirrors how the task-type bank itself is meant to be reused across topics.

The 4 multi-subtopic worked examples pool vocabulary from exactly 2 subtopics within the same topic (not cross-topic combinations, e.g. environment+urbanization), per the spec's own example phrasing ('two subtopics within one topic'). Cross-topic scenario pairing was not attempted in this pass.
